description_long: This house features a modern, boxy design with a flat roof and a series of interconnected rectangular volumes. The exterior is predominantly smooth and light-colored, with numerous windows of varying sizes distributed across the facade, some arranged in clusters and others spaced apart. The structure appears to be a single-story dwelling with a raised central section, possibly for additional ceiling height or a clerestory. The windows are mostly square or rectangular, and the main entryway is subtly integrated into the front facade, lacking prominent steps or a porch. The overall aesthetic is minimalist, with clean lines and a focus on geometric simplicity. The house is likely constructed from concrete or stucco, with glass for the windows and metal framing for structural support.
description: A modern, single-story house with a flat roof, minimalist boxy design, and numerous rectangular windows arranged asymmetrically across a smooth, light exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with a variety of window sizes and the subtle main entryway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure and the clustered window arrangement on the left volume.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying more windows and the raised central section, emphasizing the house's geometric layout.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with additional window groupings and a clear view of the flat roof sections.
attribute_entryway_design: Minimalist flush entryway with no visible steps or porch, integrated into the main facade
